metadata.description: A first-class allocatable range of IP addresses that Network.IPAddress records are carved from — the DHCP subnet pool (RFC 2131 subnet4 pools/ranges) as its own record, so allocation OWNERSHIP is expressible: which addresses are in play, which tenant/resource holds each, and whether the pool is exhausted. Same allocatable-pool pattern as Storage.Pool→Storage.Dataset (a pool is the source; the carved record depends_on it). Distinct from Network.DHCPScope (the SERVICE-side subnet config a DHCP provider realizes) and Network.IPAddress (a single CONSUMED address): the Pool is the intent-side inventory the addresses are allocated FROM.

adopts.role: allocatable-range shape: a pool is one or more contiguous address ranges within a subnet from which addresses are handed out (dynamic) or reserved (static)
prefix + ip_family shape reused from Network.IPAddress (same family, ADR-023)

lifecycle.decommission: A pool is retired only after every address allocated_from it is released; retiring a pool with live allocations must be blocked or cascade (mirrors Storage.Pool: destroying a pool destroys what was carved from it).

prefix.description: The subnet the pool draws from, as CIDR (RFC 8344), e.g. 192.0.2.0/24.

ranges.description: The allocatable ranges within the prefix (RFC 2131 subnet4 pools). Absent = the whole prefix minus exclusions is allocatable.

allocation_mode.description: How addresses leave the pool: dynamic = leased on request (DHCP), static = fixed reservations bound ahead of time, mixed = both. Aligns with Network.IPAddress.allocation on the carved record.

exclusions.description: Addresses withheld from allocation (gateway, broadcast, known-static, avoid-buggy). Not carved even if inside a range.

total_addresses.description: Count of allocatable addresses (ranges minus exclusions), once Realized.

allocated_count.description: Addresses currently allocated_from this pool (a realized fact the provider populates).

exhausted.description: True when free_count == 0 — the pool-exhaustion signal capacity/placement and quota policies read.

relationships.name: network
relationships.description: The network segment this pool serves. A pool is scoped to one L2/L3 segment (VirtualNetwork/VLAN); addresses carved from it are reachable on that segment.
